name: Indus Hospital
mission:
Friends of Indus Hospital, Inc (FOIH) is a US-based 501(c)(3) registered charity. We strive to create awareness about the healthcare needs of underprivileged patients and raise funds to provide them with equitable care. By supporting healthcare efforts in Pakistan, FOIH is helping to ensure that vulnerable individuals have access to the healthcare they need, regardless of financial or other barriers.
Through annual fundraising initiatives, community events, and awareness drives, we seek to mobilize a strong tradition of community giving and a shared commitment to changing the paradigm of healthcare delivery in Pakistan.
FOIH is an initiative taken by Americans of Pakistani origin expatriates and supported by a wider community across the USA.
All donations given to FOIH are eligible for tax deduction under US law.
scoreSummary: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc shows strong alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Friends of Indus Hospital, Inc (FOIH) is a US-based 501(c) (3) registered charity. Fulfill your Islamic obligation of Zakat with Friends of Indus Hospital. Donate Now. Donations are Tax Deductible & Zakat Eligible. Friends of Indus Hospital collects Zakat on behalf of patients treated at Indus Hospital & Health Network. Your Zakat and Sadaqah are distributed according to Islamic principles and directed to eligible programs that support patients and families in need. (Source: https://foihus.org)

dimension_explanations.alignment: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc shows high alignment for humanitarian-focused donors and is specifically designated as Zakat-eligible [1]. The organization addresses urgent healthcare needs in Pakistan with a proven track record of fundraising efficiency, spending just $0.11 per $1 raised [1][2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high impact through direct service programs including prosthetic limbs, neonatal care, and safe birth initiatives [2]. With a Charity Navigator score of 95.75/100, it maintains high standards of financial health and accountability while directing 86.8% of its $14,690,150 revenue toward its core mission [1][2].

baseline_narrative.summary: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc is a U.S.-based 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to supporting the Indus Hospital and Health Network's mission of providing premium healthcare to the underserved in Pakistan [1][2]. The organization reported a total revenue of $14,690,150 and maintains a high program expense ratio of 86.8%, ensuring the majority of funds directly support medical initiatives [2].

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The charity tracks 6 outcome metrics and holds a Platinum Candid seal, though it lacks a published Theory of Change.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: FOIH creates impact by funding the operational costs and infrastructure of the Indus Hospital & Health Network, which provides free care to those unable to pay.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that free medical services are resulting in improved patient health outcomes.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The healthcare gap in Pakistan remains vast, and FOIH's rapid growth suggests it can absorb significant additional capital.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a high <cite id="5">program expense ratio of 86.8%</cite> and spends only <cite id="6">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite>, maximizing the impact of every donation.
FOIH has earned an <cite id="10">overall score of 95.95.75/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> and holds the highest transparency rating from Candid.
The charity is governed by an <cite id="11">independent board of 14 members</cite>, ensuring robust oversight and strategic management.

strengths_deep_dive: Friends of Indus Hospital's primary strength lies in its institutional maturity and fiscal discipline. The organization has achieved a <cite id="12">3-year revenue CAGR of 24.1%</cite>, indicating rapid growth and increasing donor trust. This growth is supported by a <cite id="13">100% independent board</cite> and a perfect score of <cite id="14">100 in both Culture and Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator. Furthermore, the charity tracks <cite id="15">6 distinct outcome metrics</cite> through Candid, providing donors with verifiable evidence of the healthcare impact being delivered on the ground in Pakistan.
rich_narrative.summary: Friends of Indus Hospital Inc (FOIH) serves as a critical financial engine for the Indus Hospital and Health Network (IHHN), a massive healthcare system in Pakistan that provides <cite id="1">free, high-quality medical services</cite> to millions. The organization operates with a clear mission to <cite id="2">support the efforts of Indus Hospital</cite> in delivering care to underserved patients who would otherwise lack access to life-saving treatments. In the 2023 fiscal year, the charity demonstrated significant scale, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $14,742,480</cite>. FOIH is highly efficient in its resource allocation, maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 86.8%</cite> according to its most recent tax filings, while Charity Navigator notes a <cite id="5">program efficiency of 86.8%</cite> when evaluating its long-term financial health. The charity's fundraising model is exceptionally lean, spending only <cite id="6">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. This efficiency ensures that the vast majority of donor funds are converted into medical supplies, hospital infrastructure, and direct patient care in Pakistan. Beyond financial metrics, the organization holds a <cite id="7">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, reflecting its commitment to public accountability and outcome tracking. For Muslim donors, FOIH offers a <cite id="8">verified zakat-eligible giving channel</cite>, specifically directing funds to the <cite id="9">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> categories of asnaf. By bridging the gap between U.S.-based donors and the healthcare needs of Pakistan's most vulnerable populations, FOIH has established itself as a premier institution in the global health sector.
